company: Hua Hong Grace Semiconductor (formerly Hua Hong Semiconductor)
newsSinceJun6: No material new fundamentals in the Jun 6-9 window. Context: company completed rebrand to Hua Hong Grace Semiconductor (announced ~Jun 3, cosmetic); Q1 2026 beat with raised Q2 guidance (US$690-700M revenue, 14-16% GM) and the $1.2B HLMC consolidation are older catalysts already in the price. Stock has pulled back ~10-12% from its post-earnings peak (~HK$156.5 on Jun 5 to HK$137.1 Jun 8), bouncing +2.2% to HK$140.1 today - looks like profit-taking/flow, not a thesis break.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - Hua Hong Q1 beat and raised guidance, stock up 12.3%
CNBC quote - Hua Hong Grace Semiconductor (1347-HK), name change and price history
Dealroom - Hua Hong consolidates domestic capacity with $1.2B HLMC acquisition
Futu News - Hua Hong 1347.HK: promising 2026 with AI spillover and price hikes

verdicts.rankRationale: On a CPO-lens alpha page Hua Hong is an indirect China-localization proxy with low direct CPO exposure; the ~12% pullback from the post-earnings peak with the thesis intact modestly restores asymmetry, but with +72% YTD and no fresh CPO-relevant evidence since Jun 6 there is no basis to re-rank a day after yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Raised Q2 guidance, mature-node price hikes with AI spillover demand, and the HLMC consolidation add capacity and profitability while the 10-12% pullback offers a cheaper entry into the China-localization bottleneck story.
verdicts.neutral: Quality localization play, but on this page its CPO relevance is second-order; after a 72% YTD rerating the residual alpha is largely a valuation-reset trade, not a thesis upgrade.
verdicts.bear: China mature-node pricing remains structurally oversupplied; 14-16% gross margin guidance leaves little cushion, and the post-earnings fade suggests the rerating ran ahead of fundamentals.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results confirming the raised guidance plus evidence of sustained mature-node price increases and HLMC integration accretion.
verdicts.primaryRisk: China mature-node pricing pressure and oversupply compressing the 14-16% gross margin guidance after a 72% YTD run.
